title: Graceful degradation can return fallback portfolio while dropping higher-level constraints
evidence: functions_python/services/portfolio/optimizer_core.py:811-886 builds fallback weights after solver failure and explicitly states Level 4/5 constraints are lost; optimizer_core.py:1175-1220 returns status fallback/optimal with warnings=[]; frontend/src/hooks/usePortfolioActions.ts:623-646 accepts optimal/fallback and opens the review/tactical flow.
impact: Can produce an applicable portfolio that no longer respects geo/group/tactical/objective constraints, contaminating final recommendations if the user accepts it.
recommendation: Populate blocking warnings for every fallback path, expose lost_constraints, and require explicit confirmation or strict-mode block when constraints beyond basic risk buckets are dropped.

title: Volatility band/risk budget is modeled but not enforced as a hard constraint
evidence: functions_python/services/portfolio/constraints_builder_v1.py:158-175 builds vol_band; optimizer_core.py:739-745 only uses target_vol for efficient_risk; optimizer_core.py:1179-1193 reports target/achieved vol but does not enforce vol_band min/max.
impact: Profiles may appear to have volatility caps while the solver only optimizes an objective or target, allowing portfolios outside intended risk policy.
recommendation: Either enforce vol_band as a hard/soft post-check with status degradation or rename it as reporting-only target. Add tests for achieved_vol outside band.

title: Feasibility precheck misses max-attainable bucket floors under max_weight/fixed positions
evidence: functions_python/services/portfolio/feasibility_precheck.py:146-175 only blocks when max exposure in a bucket is zero; optimizer_core.py:989-1013 trusts this precheck before solver instantiation.
impact: A bucket minimum can be mathematically unreachable even when one fund has nonzero exposure, causing solver failure or fallback portfolios that do not meet the requested policy.
recommendation: Extend precheck to calculate maximum attainable exposure using sorted bucket exposures, max_weight, locked positions, and remaining budget.

title: Missing V2 exposure can be treated as zero equity during suitability checks
evidence: functions_python/services/portfolio/suitability_engine.py:15-19 requires classification_v2 but does not require usable exposure; lines 27-39 set real_eq from get_v2_asset_mix(...), defaulting missing exposure to 0.
impact: A fund with incomplete exposure may pass conservative suitability because missing exposure becomes 0% equity, especially if flags/risk_bucket are incomplete.
recommendation: For risk profiles 1-4, require usable V2 exposure or apply conservative classification-based blocks. Surface missing exposure as a warning/block, not zero exposure.

title: Weight normalization can preserve negative output weights if upstream weights contain negatives
evidence: functions_python/services/portfolio/utils.py:27-31 computes denominator with max(0,v) but returns raw v/s; optimizer_core.py:821-824 normalizes ef.clean_weights output without a second non-negative assertion.
impact: A tiny negative or unexpected upstream weight can leak into final weights despite a long-only solver contract.
recommendation: Normalize from sanitized non-negative values, assert all weights >= 0, assert finite values, and fail/fallback with explicit warning if invariant breaks.

title: Group exposure constraints can fallback to derived/ms/legacy sources without surfaced source or warning
evidence: functions_python/services/portfolio/utils.py:369-473 reads V2 group maps, then identity, then derived/ms/meta legacy maps; optimizer_core.py:533-560 uses these vectors for geo/group constraints.
impact: Reporting and optimizer constraints can be based on mixed-quality region/sector/credit sources without the user seeing which source was used.
recommendation: Return source diagnostics for group vectors and add result warnings when constraints rely on derived/ms/legacy fallback.

title: Mixed-scale or malformed asset_mix is normalized silently in optimizer reader
evidence: functions_python/services/portfolio/utils.py:134-138 converts values >1.5 to decimals; lines 245-284 normalizes the final vector; SEM-4 documents the same runtime contract.
impact: Optimizer math is robust for known 0-1/0-100 cohorts, but malformed or mixed-scale vectors can be masked and become hard-to-audit exposures.
recommendation: Add read-time diagnostics: detected_scale, normalized_sum, input_sum, source path, and mixed-scale warning. Do not change storage in this audit.

title: Successful optimizer responses return warnings=[] even when fallbacks or degradations occurred
evidence: functions_python/services/portfolio/optimizer_core.py:1170 records relaxed_constraints in explainability; line 1220 always returns warnings=[] for successful result; _run_solver logs fallback attempts at lines 760-805.
impact: Users and downstream systems may miss solver fallback, lost constraints, legacy fallback, or tactical fallback unless they inspect explainability/logs.
recommendation: Populate warnings with solver_path, fallback_reason, lost_constraints, legacy_fallback_counts, tactical_bl_fallback, and data_readiness gaps.

title: Frontend display can truncate, round, and filter optimizer weights without renormalizing
evidence: frontend/src/hooks/usePortfolioActions.ts:176-191 multiplies backend decimals by 100, rounds to 2 decimals, filters <=0.01%, and adds auto assets without final displayed renormalization.
impact: Displayed/applied proposed portfolio can sum away from 100 or hide tiny allocations, creating optimizer_output vs displayed_output drift.
recommendation: After mapping backend weights, renormalize displayed weights to 100 or show residual/unallocated cash explicitly. Add a UI invariant test.

title: Tactical views can fall back to mean/covariance while explainability still implies tactical impact
evidence: optimizer_core.py:343-372 falls back from Black-Litterman to pairwise mean/covariance on exception; optimizer_core.py:1172 reports tactical_views_impact based only on tactical_views truthiness; quant_core.py:110-124 filters views by ticker in prior returns.
impact: A tactical override may not actually affect mu/S, while output can still say tactical views were applied.
recommendation: Track tactical_views_requested_count, applied_count, dropped_count, and bl_fallback_reason. Only claim impact if at least one view survived and BL succeeded.

title: Optimizer runtime has optional Firestore side effects during normal execution
evidence: functions_python/services/data_fetcher.py:247-345 reads and writes system_settings/risk_free_rate cache; endpoints_portfolio.py:421-465 writes optimizer_snapshots when save_snapshot/snapshot_label is supplied.
impact: Optimization is not fully side-effect-free, which complicates read-only expectations and reproducibility in audit or dry-run modes.
recommendation: Add an explicit dry_run/no_write flag for optimizer calls or separate cache/snapshot writes from the quantitative run.

title: Swap recommendation layer can use universal fallback and commercial/retrocession ranking
evidence: frontend/src/utils/fundSwapper.ts:187-203 adds universal fallback candidates and scores commercialScore; lines 224-248 select a second option by different company/retrocession within quality floor; directSearch.ts:134-146 can sort by maximizeRetro.
impact: Recommendation output may be commercially biased or taxonomically broad without enough user-visible disclosure or backend suitability validation.
recommendation: Surface recommendation tier, commercial factor, and suitability/concentration checks. Keep backend as authority for final recommendation filtering.

title: Optimizer result lacks deterministic run_id/input hash and complete source stamps
evidence: optimizer_core.py:1195-1221 returns metrics/explainability but no run_id, input hash, price cache version, RF source, or code contract version; data_fetcher.py:247-345 can use cached/ECB/fallback RF.
impact: Harder to reproduce an optimizer result exactly after data/cache/RF changes.
recommendation: Add run_id, request_hash, universe_hash, price_as_of, rf_source, rf_rate_timestamp, optimizer_version, and constraints_version to result/explainability.

title: Local SmartPortfolio remains a presentation-only fallback that can still generate portfolios
evidence: frontend/src/utils/rulesEngine.ts:7-13 states backend is authority; lines 462-647 generate local portfolios with local risk profile seed; lines 650-682 fallback to local seed if backend business rules fail.
impact: Can confuse UX previews with backend optimizer authority, though code comments already warn it is presentation-only.
recommendation: Label local portfolios as preview-only in telemetry/UI and avoid mixing local scores with backend optimizer outputs.
